flags.name: verbose

flags.help: Show which checks would run based on current filters without executing them

flags.help: Directory containing migration files to read or write
flags.name: dir

flags.help: Preview the migration SQL statements without executing
flags.name: dry-run

flags.help: Advisory lock acquisition timeout in seconds before aborting
flags.name: timeout

apply.help: Apply all pending migrations to the target database in order. Each migration runs inside its own transaction with advisory locking to prevent concurrent execution. Non-transactional operations like CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY execute outside transactions automatically. Use --dry-run to preview the SQL without executing.
apply.name: apply

flags.help: PostgreSQL connection URL for the target database server
flags.name: db

flags.help: Version label for the baseline record
flags.name: version

flags.help: Human-readable description
flags.name: description

baseline.help: Mark an existing database as being at a specific migration version without executing any migration SQL. Use this when adopting pgdesign migrations for a database whose schema was already created by other means. Idempotent: re-running with the same version succeeds; a different version errors.
baseline.name: baseline

args.help: Path to TOML schema file(s) or directory containing them
args.name: path

flags.help: PostgreSQL connection URL for the target database server
flags.name: db

flags.help: Semantic version string for the generated migration
flags.name: version

flags.help: Directory containing migration files to read or write
flags.name: dir

generate.help: Generate versioned migration files by comparing the TOML schema against a live database. Produces up and down SQL files with risk annotations, safety linting, and expand-migrate-contract phase classification. Volatile defaults and operations on large tables are automatically detected and handled safely.
generate.name: generate

args.help: Path to TOML schema file(s) or directory containing them
args.name: path

flags.help: PostgreSQL connection URL for the target database server
flags.name: db

flags.help: Directory containing migration files to read or write
flags.name: dir

plan.help: Plan migrations by diffing the TOML schema against a live database without writing any files. Shows which tables, columns, indexes, and constraints would change, along with risk levels and required lock types for each operation. Useful for previewing changes before generating migration files.
plan.name: plan

flags.help: PostgreSQL connection URL for the target database server
flags.name: db

flags.help: Directory containing migration files to read or write
flags.name: dir

flags.help: Target version to rollback to (exclusive — this version stays applied)
flags.name: to

rollback.help: Rollback applied database migrations to a specified target version. Executes down migration SQL in reverse application order with advisory locking. Multi-step rollbacks verify reversibility of all steps before starting. The target version is exclusive, meaning that version stays applied after rollback completes.
rollback.name: rollback

flags.help: First migration version to include in the squash range
flags.name: from

flags.help: Last migration version to include in the squash range
flags.name: to

flags.help: Directory containing migration files to read or write
flags.name: dir

flags.help: PostgreSQL connection URL for pre-squash safety check
flags.name: db

squash.help: Consolidate a range of sequential migration files into a single optimized migration. Recognizes 12 types of inverse operation pairs for cancellation, merges sequential type changes, and folds column additions into CREATE TABLE statements where possible. The original migration files are replaced with one combined migration file.
squash.name: squash

flags.help: PostgreSQL connection URL for the target database server
flags.name: db

flags.help: Directory containing migration files to read or write
flags.name: dir

status.help: Show which migrations have been applied to the target database and which are still pending. Reads the migration tracking table and compares it with the migrations directory to display version numbers, applied timestamps, and current execution status for each migration file.
status.name: status

flags.help: PostgreSQL connection URL for the staging test database
flags.name: db

flags.help: Directory containing migration files to read or write
flags.name: dir

flags.help: Maximum time in seconds before the test run is aborted
flags.name: timeout

flags.help: Test by replaying migrations into a shadow database and diffing against TOML schema
flags.name: shadow

test.help: Test migrations by applying them against a staging database to verify correctness before production deployment. With --shadow mode, replays all migrations into a fresh database and diffs the result against the TOML schema to catch drift between migration files and schema definitions.
test.name: test

migrate.help: Database migration planning, generation, and execution
migrate.name: migrate

flags.help: PostgreSQL connection URL for the target database server
flags.name: db

flags.help: Drop databases older than this duration (e.g., 2h, 30m)
flags.name: older-than

gc.help: Drop orphaned test databases that were not properly torn down after test runs. Scans the PostgreSQL server for databases matching the pgdesign test naming pattern and removes those older than the specified duration. Useful for cleaning up after interrupted or failed test runs in CI and local development.
gc.name: gc

flags.help: Target programming language(s) for wrapper generation
flags.name: language

flags.help: Name of the SQL output section (for disambiguation)
flags.name: output

flags.help: Overwrite existing wrapper files without prompting
flags.name: force-overwrite

flags.help: CI provider for workflow generation (e.g., github-actions)
flags.name: ci

init.help: Generate test database wrapper code for consumer projects that need to run integration tests against a pgdesign-managed schema. Produces language-specific helper modules with setup and teardown functions that create ephemeral databases, apply DDL, and clean up automatically after each test run.
init.name: init

flags.help: PostgreSQL connection URL for the target database server
flags.name: db

flags.help: Path to the SQL DDL file to apply to the test database
flags.name: ddl

setup.help: Create an ephemeral test database on the PostgreSQL server and apply the specified DDL schema to it. The database is created with a unique name containing a timestamp and random suffix to allow parallel test execution. Returns the connection URL for the new database.
setup.name: setup

flags.help: PostgreSQL connection URL for the target database server
flags.name: db

teardown.help: Drop an ephemeral test database that was previously created by testdb setup. Terminates any remaining connections to the database before dropping it. Should be called in test cleanup to prevent orphaned databases from accumulating on the PostgreSQL server over time.
teardown.name: teardown

testdb.help: Manage ephemeral test databases for schema testing
testdb.name: testdb
